title: Transformation of Bacillus subtilis with DNA obtained from natural donor cell lysis
author: James P. Finn IV

steps_raw.instruction: Streak out the donor strain onto a LB agar plate with appropriate antibiotics and incubate at 37 °C for approximately 18 hours to obtain fresh, single colonies.
Streak out the recipient strain onto a LB agar plate and incubate at 30 °C for, at most, 42 hours to obtain fresh, single colonies in two days.
If the recipient was not streaked out yesterday, then streak out the recipient onto a LB agar plate with appropriate antibiotics and incubate at 37 °C for fresh, single colonies about 18 hours later.
In 18mm sterile, glass test tubes, prepare 1 mL, 1x MC medium for each donor .
Inoculate 1 mL, 1x MC medium with one donor colony.

Alternatively, standard 96-well plates may be used with 200 µL of 1x MC medium per well or square deep-well, 96-well plates may be used with 400 µL of 1x MC medium per well. Note: ensure there is enough donor culture for the number of unique transformations.

Table for making 1 mL or 400 µL of 1x MC medium. A B 1 900 µL sterile ddH2O 360 µL sterile ddH2O 2 3 µL 1M MgSO4 1.2 µL 1M MgSO4 3 100 µL 10x MC (stored at -30°C) 40 µL 10x MC (stored at -30°C) 4 1 mL of 1x MC 400 µL of 1x MC Column A is for 1 mL of 1x MC medium. Column B is for 400 µL of 1x MC medium.
Vortex at setting 3 to 4 for 10 seconds and remove the inoculating stick. 00:00:10, Vortex
Alternatively, if using 96-well plates, then use a pipette tip to inoculate each well with a colony.

Incubate donor culture at 37 °C with aeration. Note: 18 mm test tubes can aerate rolling in a roller drum. Note: 96-well plates should have an aeration seal applied and aerate on a platform shaker at 350 rpm.
Incubate for 20-24 hours to obtain sufficient cell lysis. 24:00:00, Donor Incubation
Four hours before the end of the 20-24 hours incubation time for the donor, prepare 1 mL, 1x MC medium for the recipient in sterile, 18mm glass test tubes.
Inoculate 1 mL, 1x MC medium with one recipient colony using a sterile inoculation stick.
Incubate the recipient cultures at 37 °C for 4 hours with aeration. Use the same aeration methods as used for the donor cultures. go to step #7 04:00:00, Recipient Incubation
Shortly before 4 hours have elapsed, prepare the donor for transformation: Is it necessary to filter the donor? (see Before Start)
Donor DNA source may sit at room temperature while preparing the transformation tubes, but it can be stored at -30 °C .

The recipient can be grown in either glass test tubes or 96-well plates: Will the recipient be grown in Test Tubes or 96-well plates? (Select from the first step case below and continue within that step case for the remainder of the protocol.)
Incubate the recipient cultures at 37 °C for 4 hours with aeration. Use the same aeration methods as used for the donor cultures. go to step #7 04:00:00, Recipient Incubation
If filtration is necessary (see Before Start):
If working in 18 mm glass test tubes:
Aliquot 1 mL sample of the donor to a 1.7-microfuge tube.
Spin the sample at 21,000 x g in a centrifuge for 1 minute. Remove supernatant to a new microfuge tube. Take care not to disrupt cells and cell debris in pellet.
Filter the supernatant through a 0.22 µm PES filter. This filtrate is the source of donor DNA. Note: cellulose acetate and nylon filters are equally effective, but PES is usually most cost effective.
Donor DNA source may sit at room temperature while preparing the transformation tubes.
Is the donor growing in test tube or 96-well plate format?
Filter away from the donor bacteria with a 0.22 µm filter type. This filtrate is the source of donor DNA.
Donor DNA source may sit at room temperature while preparing the transformation tubes, but it can be stored at -30 °C .
Aliquot 1 mL sample of the donor to a 1.7-microfuge tube.
Spin the sample at 21,000 x g (max speed) in a centrifuge for 1 minute. 21000 x g, °C, 1 minute, Spin the donor sample
Remove supernatant to a new microfuge tube. Take care not to disrupt cells and cell debris in pellet.
Filter the supernatant through a 0.22 µm PES filter. This filtrate is the source of donor DNA. Note: cellulose acetate and nylon filters are equally effective, but PES is usually most cost effective.
Inoculate each well of a 96-well plate with 200 µL, 1x MC medium with recipient . Note: A deep-well, 96-well plate can be used for the recipient growth with 400 µL of 1x MC medium.
If using one recipient , then inoculate each well with 200 µL of 1x MC medium with recipient .
If using many unique recipients , then use a pipette tip to inoculate each well with one colony.
Shortly before 4 hours have elapsed, prepare the donor for transformation: Is it necessary to filter the donor? (see Before Start)
Is the donor growing in test tube or 96-well plate format?
Aliquot 1 mL sample of the donor to a 1.7-microfuge tube.
Spin the sample at 21,000 x g (max speed) in a centrifuge for 1 minute. 21000 x g, °C, 1 minute, Spin the donor sample
Remove supernatant to a new microfuge tube. Take care not to disrupt cells and cell debris in pellet.
Filter the supernatant through a 0.22 µm PES filter. This filtrate is the source of donor DNA. Note: cellulose acetate and nylon filters are equally effective, but PES is usually most cost effective.
Donor DNA source may sit at room temperature while preparing the transformation tubes, but it can be stored at -30 °C .

Aspirate directly from the donor culture and use this as the source of donor DNA . Note: This aspiration of the donor can happen from either a test tubes or a 96-well plate. Note: Due to the selective pressure, no spin is necessary.
Filter away from the donor bacteria with a 0.22 µm filter type. This filtrate is the source of donor DNA.
Donor DNA source may sit at room temperature while preparing the transformation tubes, but it can be stored at -30 °C .
Transfer 200 µL of the recipient to a sterile, 13mm glass tube (this is the transformation tube).
Transfer 10 µL of donor filtrate to the transformation tube.
Transfer 200 µL of the recipient to a sterile, 13mm glass tube (this is the transformation tube).
Transfer 10 µL of donor lysate to the transformation tube.
Is the donor growing in test tube or 96-well plate format?

Aspirate directly from the donor culture and use this as the source of donor DNA . Note: This aspiration of the donor can happen from either a test tubes or a 96-well plate. Note: Due to the selective pressure, no spin is necessary.
Transfer 200 µL of the recipient to a fresh well of a 96-well plate.
Transfer 10 µL of donor lysate to the transformation well.
Transfer 200 µL of the recipient to a fresh well of a 96-well plate.
Transfer 10 µL of donor filtrate to the transformation well.
Place transformations at 37 °C for 2 hours with aeration. Use the same aeration methods as used for the donor cultures. go to step #7 02:00:00, Transformation Growth
Dilute transformation cultures 1:10 and 1:100.
Use 4-5 beads per petri plate to spread the cells evenly. Note: do not allow the liquid to sit on the agar plate for too long before spreading.
Incubate plates at 37 °C overnight, 30 °C for two days, or Room temperature for three days. Note: the resulting colonies should be the desired transformants.
Dispense 100 µL to 200 µL on appropriate selective plates. Note: ensure the agar plates are not so dry that they prevent even spreading.

Place transformations at 37 °C for 2 hours with aeration. Use the same aeration methods as used for the donor cultures. go to step #7 Note: Use the large 18 mm tubes with small caps or 1 mL pipette tips inside to hold the small 13 mm glass tubes (See Figure 1 in Loyo & Burton 2018, link in References Section ). 02:00:00, Transformation Growth
Dilute transformation cultures 1:10 and 1:100.
Dispense 100 µL to 200 µL on appropriate selective plates. Note: ensure the agar plates are not so dry that they prevent even spreading.
Use 4-5 beads per petri plate to spread the cells evenly. Note: do not allow the liquid to sit on the agar plate for too long before spreading.
Incubate plates at 37 °C overnight, 30 °C for two days, or Room temperature for three days. Note: the resulting colonies should be the desired transformants.

Place transformations at 37 °C for 2 hours with aeration. Use the same aeration methods as used for the donor cultures. go to step #7 Note: Use the large 18 mm tubes with small caps or 1 mL pipette tips inside to hold the small 13 mm glass tubes (See Figure 1 in Loyo & Burton 2018, link in References Section ). 02:00:00, Transformation Growth
Dilute transformation cultures 1:10 and 1:100.
Dispense 100 µL to 200 µL on appropriate selective plates. Note: ensure the agar plates are not so dry that they prevent even spreading.
Use 4-5 beads per petri plate to spread the cells evenly. Note: do not allow the liquid to sit on the agar plate for too long before spreading.
Incubate plates at 37 °C overnight, 30 °C for two days, or Room temperature for three days. Note: the resulting colonies should be the desired transformants.
Place transformations at 37 °C for 2 hours with aeration. Use the same aeration methods as used for the donor cultures. go to step #7 02:00:00, Transformation Growth
Dilute transformation cultures 1:10 and 1:100.
Dispense 100 µL to 200 µL on appropriate selective plates. Note: ensure the agar plates are not so dry that they prevent even spreading.
Use 4-5 beads per petri plate to spread the cells evenly. Note: do not allow the liquid to sit on the agar plate for too long before spreading.
Incubate plates at 37 °C overnight, 30 °C for two days, or Room temperature for three days. Note: the resulting colonies should be the desired transformants.
In wells that contain 200 µL of the recipient , add 10 µL of donor filtrate to the transformation well.
Place transformations at 37 °C for 2 hours with aeration. Use the same aeration methods as used for the donor cultures. go to step #7 02:00:00, Transformation Growth

Incubate plates at 37 °C overnight, 30 °C for two days, or Room temperature for three days. Note: the resulting colonies should be the desired transformants.
In wells that contain 200 µL of the recipient , add 10 µL of donor filtrate to the transformation well.

Incubate plates at 37 °C overnight, 30 °C for two days, or Room temperature for three days. Note: the resulting colonies should be the desired transformants.

Incubate plates at 37 °C overnight, 30 °C for two days, or Room temperature for three days. Note: the resulting colonies should be the desired transformants.

Place transformations at 37 °C for 2 hours with aeration. Use the same aeration methods as used for the donor cultures. go to step #7 02:00:00, Transformation Growth

Dispense 100 µL to 200 µL on appropriate selective plates. Note: ensure the agar plates are not so dry that they prevent even spreading.
Use 4-5 beads per petri plate to spread the cells evenly. Note: do not allow the liquid to sit on the agar plate for too long before spreading.
Incubate plates at 37 °C overnight, 30 °C for two days, or Room temperature for three days. Note: the resulting colonies should be the desired transformants.

Table for making 1 mL or 400 µL of 1x MC medium. A B 1 900 µL sterile ddH2O 360 µL sterile ddH2O 2 3 µL 1M MgSO4 1.2 µL 1M MgSO4 3 100 µL 10x MC (stored at -30°C) 40 µL 10x MC (stored at -30°C) 4 1 mL of 1x MC 400 µL of 1x MC Column A is for 1 mL of 1x MC medium. Column B is for 400 µL of 1x MC medium.

Alternatively, standard 96-well plates may be used with 200 µL of 1x MC medium per well or square deep-well, 96-well plates may be used with 400 µL of 1x MC medium per well. Note: ensure there is enough recipient culture for the number of unique transformations.
